target.benchmark: bun scripts/bench/world-stream.ts  (5 km/s default; HAYSTACK_RENDERED_LIMIT sets field size)
target.notes: Thresholds are knobs. The data backend is fair game (see RALPH.md). publishAll p95 = server per-tick broadcast cost across all peers.

baselineAtHead.interpretation: Per-tick cost ~O(players x field). Dominant costs: per-peer getSnapshot + JSON.stringify-hash of every snapshot key incl. the 100k asteroid array; cubic cell scan on crossings; full-field re-serialization into deltas.

items.name: Benchmark harness
items.spec: DONE. scripts/bench/world-stream.ts drives publishAll() in-process against N simulated peers at 5 km/s, prints JSON metrics (publishAll p50/p95/max, MB/s per peer, peak RSS, effective visible count) for a {players}x{renderedLimit} matrix. HAYSTACK_RENDERED_LIMIT made env-configurable. PASS: runs green for {players:[1,4,16], visibleAsteroids:[2000,100000]}.

items.name: Baseline + profiled bottleneck
items.spec: DONE. Baseline captured (see baselineAtHead + progress.txt). Top costs identified: (a) per-peer per-tick getSnapshot rebuild + JSON.stringify change-detection over the full field, (b) cubic cell scan on cell crossings, (c) full 100k field re-serialized into deltas. PASS: baseline numbers + ranked bottleneck list recorded.

items.name: Shared world computed once per tick (sub-linear in players)
items.spec: World state shared across players (field, structures, other ships, change-detection) is computed/serialized at most once per tick, not once per peer. PASS: in the benchmark, going 1 -> 16 players at a fixed field size increases publishAll per-tick time by far less than 16x; the per-player marginal cost is bounded by per-player view/delta work only. Record the scaling curve.

items.name: Change-detection is not O(field)
items.spec: Per-peer per-tick change detection must not JSON.stringify (or otherwise touch) the whole field every tick. Use dirty/version tracking, structural sharing, or equivalent. PASS: at {players:1, visibleAsteroids:100000, 5 km/s}, publishAll p50 drops well below the baseline 90 ms and change-detection cost does not grow with visibleAsteroids when the field is unchanged.

items.name: Field re-paging is incremental (bandwidth + cubic-scan)
items.spec: A 5 km/s player crossing cells must not re-send the full 100k field nor rerun an O(field) cubic scan per crossing. Send only added/removed rocks (or derive the field client-side), and make the field query incremental/indexed. PASS: at {players:1, visibleAsteroids:100000, 5 km/s}, MB/s per peer drops by >=1 order of magnitude vs baseline (261 MB/s) AND publishAll p95 (which baseline shows spiking to ~419 ms on crossings) drops well under the single-player threshold.

items.name: Single player @ 100k @ 5 km/s under budget
items.spec: PASS: benchmark at {players:1, visibleAsteroids:100000, 5 km/s} shows effective client-visible count >=100000 AND publishAll p95 < 8 ms (knob).

items.name: Target concurrent players @ 100k @ 5 km/s under budget
items.spec: PASS: benchmark at {players: target.concurrentPlayers, visibleAsteroids:100000, 5 km/s} shows publishAll p95 < tickBudgetMs/2 (~16 ms, knob) AND no overrun ticks over the run.

items.name: No functional/quality regression at scale
items.spec: PASS: `bun run verify` green (minus documented pre-existing fails); e2e screenshot/multiplayer/ui green; and a checked validation confirms at high density that mining, a surface/pocket scan, asteroid/deposit discovery, multiplayer remote-position sync, and chat all still work, and a focused client applies world-stream updates at ~30 Hz. Ship/cargo/credits/structures/discoveries still survive a server restart.

items.name: Orders-of-magnitude summary
items.spec: PASS: progress.txt contains a before/after table (baseline vs final) and the achieved multiple — target: publishAll cost at {16 players, 100k, 5 km/s} reduced from ~2167 ms to under ~16 ms (>=100x), and MB/s per peer cut by >=1 order of magnitude. If the realized gain is less, state the measured number and the binding limit honestly.
